blockingIssues.description: cache_type='memory' and ram_cache_maxsize on separate lines in convex.py. The test_no_unbounded_memory_caches test scans source files line-by-line and requires both to appear on the same line. Lines 52 and 97 in y/prices/convex.py had them split across lines, causing the cachebox bounds test to fail.
blockingIssues.suggestedFix: Put cache_type='memory' and ram_cache_maxsize on the same line in both @a_sync.a_sync decorators in y/prices/convex.py. Fixed in commit 86cf701c.

assertionChecks.description: Dynamic Convex detection via operator() + booster verification
assertionChecks.evidence: is_convex_lp() calls get_underlying_lp() which calls operator() on the token, verifies the returned booster has poolLength(), then iterates poolInfo(i) to find the matching token. Test test_dynamic_token_is_convex passes for cvxsteCRV (not in hardcoded list).

assertionChecks.description: Previously working hardcoded Convex tokens still work
assertionChecks.evidence: Static MAPPING lookup is the fast path. test_hardcoded_convex_price and test_hardcoded_token_is_convex both pass for cvx3crv.

assertionChecks.description: Full test suite passes after Convex
assertionChecks.evidence: test_no_unbounded_memory_caches FAILED due to cache_type/ram_cache_maxsize split across lines. All 11 convex-specific tests pass. Fix applied in commit 86cf701c.

assertionChecks.description: stuck_coro_debugger on new async pricing functions
assertionChecks.evidence: @stuck_coro_debugger applied to is_convex_lp (line 56), get_underlying_lp (line 94), and get_price (line 195). All three new/modified async functions are properly decorated.

codeQualityObservations.description: Two-stage detection (static fast path + dynamic fallback) is well-structured and clearly documented.
Comprehensive test coverage: 11 tests covering static path, dynamic detection, underlying LP resolution, pricing, PriceResult format, and non-convex tokens.
Error handling via return_None_on_failure and call_reverted pattern is consistent with codebase conventions.
cache_type='memory' and ram_cache_maxsize split across decorator lines violated the cachebox bounds test. Style: put them on the same line (or use single-line form) to satisfy the linter test.

summary: The convex-dynamic-detection feature is functionally correct and well-tested. The dynamic detection via operator() + booster verification works correctly. One blocking issue was found: cache_type='memory' and ram_cache_maxsize were on separate decorator lines, causing the cachebox bounds test to fail. This was fixed in commit 86cf701c immediately after discovery.
